binaries.title: modem.sys (Windows Unimodem driver) - Missing zero-initialization of PDO-name pool buffer in ModemHandleSymbolicLink (CWE-908 hardening)

changed_functions.name: ModemHandleSymbolicLink
key_changes: ADDED: memset (0x1C0001A40) call to zero-initialize DeviceName.Buffer (0x202 bytes) AFTER the second ExAllocatePoolWithTag allocation and BEFORE IoGetDeviceProperty(DevicePropertyPhysicalDeviceObjectName). In the unpatched build this second buffer is allocated but not zeroed. The first buffer (Destination) is zeroed via memset in both builds.

Register reallocation: pool-type argument for the second allocation changed from 'mov ecx, 1' to 'lea ecx, [rdi-1]' (both compute ecx=1=PagedPool) and the ResultLength-2 subtraction changed from 'sub ax, 2' to 'mov edi, 2; sub ax, di' (both compute the same value). rdi is repurposed to hold the constant 2 used in the new memset size computation (MaximumLength + 2 = 0x202).
Jump targets shift by ~0x14 bytes throughout the function due to the inserted memset sequence. No behavioral change.
changed_functions.security_notes: ModemHandleSymbolicLink creates the modem device's \DosDevices\ symbolic link and registers the device interface. In create mode (a3 != 0) it allocates two 0x202-byte PagedPool 'UMDM' buffers: (1) Destination.Buffer for '\DosDevices\<FriendlyName>', which IS zeroed via memset (0x1C0007736) in both builds, and (2) DeviceName.Buffer for the PDO name (DevicePropertyPhysicalDeviceObjectName), which is NOT zeroed in the unpatched build. The patch adds memset(DeviceName.Buffer, 0, 0x202) at 0x1C00077E1-0x1C00077EE. This is CWE-908 (Use of Uninitialized Resource) hardening. There is no demonstrable information disclosure: DeviceName.Length is set to ResultLength-2, IoCreateSymbolicLink consumes only Length bytes of the buffer as the link target, and the buffer is freed immediately afterward. The bytes beyond Length are never read or copied to any caller-observable location, so the added memset is defense-in-depth that brings the second allocation in line with the already-zeroed first allocation.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED (second allocation, no memset before IoGetDeviceProperty):
00000001C00077A9  mov     edx, 202h                ; NumberOfBytes
00000001C00077AE  mov     dword ptr [rbp+DeviceName.Length], 2000000h  ; Length=0, MaximumLength=0x200
00000001C00077B5  mov     ecx, 1                   ; PoolType = PagedPool
00000001C00077BA  mov     r8d, 4D444D55h           ; Tag 'UMDM'
00000001C00077C0  call    cs:__imp_ExAllocatePoolWithTag
00000001C00077CC  mov     [rbp+DeviceName.Buffer], rax
00000001C00077D0  test    rax, rax
00000001C00077D3  jnz     short loc_1C00077DF
00000001C00077D5  mov     edi, 0C000009Ah
00000001C00077DA  jmp     loc_1C00078AF
00000001C00077DF  movzx   r8d, [rbp+DeviceName.MaximumLength]  ; BufferLength = 0x200
00000001C00077E4  lea     rcx, [rbp+arg_18]        ; &ResultLength
00000001C00077E8  mov     [rsp+50h+ResultLength], rcx
00000001C00077ED  mov     r9, rax                  ; PropertyBuffer (uninitialized)
00000001C00077F0  mov     rcx, rsi                 ; DeviceObject
00000001C00077F3  mov     edx, 0Bh                 ; DevicePropertyPhysicalDeviceObjectName
00000001C00077F8  call    cs:__imp_IoGetDeviceProperty

PATCHED (memset inserted between allocation and IoGetDeviceProperty):
00000001C00077C2  call    cs:__imp_ExAllocatePoolWithTag
00000001C00077CE  mov     [rbp+DeviceName.Buffer], rax
00000001C00077D2  test    rax, rax
00000001C00077D5  jnz     short loc_1C00077E1
00000001C00077D7  mov     edi, 0C000009Ah
00000001C00077DC  jmp     loc_1C00078C3
00000001C00077E1  movzx   r8d, [rbp+DeviceName.MaximumLength]  ; 0x200
00000001C00077E6  xor     edx, edx                 ; Val = 0
00000001C00077E8  add     r8, rdi                  ; Size = MaximumLength + 2 = 0x202
00000001C00077EB  mov     rcx, rax                 ; buffer pointer
00000001C00077EE  call    memset                   ; THE FIX
00000001C00077F3  movzx   r8d, [rbp+DeviceName.MaximumLength] ; BufferLength = 0x200
00000001C00077F8  lea     rax, [rbp+arg_18]        ; &ResultLength
00000001C00077FC  mov     r9, [rbp+DeviceName.Buffer]        ; PropertyBuffer (now zeroed)
00000001C0007800  mov     edx, 0Bh                 ; DevicePropertyPhysicalDeviceObjectName
00000001C0007805  mov     rcx, rsi                 ; DeviceObject
00000001C0007808  mov     [rsp+50h+ResultLength], rax
00000001C000780D  call    cs:__imp_IoGetDeviceProperty

security_findings.entry_point: PnP device setup: ModemAddDevice (0x1C0006B60) and ModemPnP (0x1C0006EF0) call ModemHandleSymbolicLink during device enumeration. Not reachable from an untrusted IOCTL surface.
call_chain: PnP Manager calls ModemAddDevice (0x1C0006B60) when a modem device is enumerated
UNPATCHED: no zeroing of DeviceName.Buffer; PATCHED: memset(DeviceName.Buffer, 0, 0x202)
IoGetDeviceProperty(PDO, DevicePropertyPhysicalDeviceObjectName, 0x200, DeviceName.Buffer, &ResultLength) partially fills the buffer
IoCreateSymbolicLink(&Destination, &DeviceName) consumes only DeviceName.Length bytes as the link target
ExFreePoolWithTag(DeviceName.Buffer, 0) frees the buffer; the tail is never read
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Use of Uninitialized Resource (CWE-908) - hardening; no demonstrable disclosure
security_findings.description: In the unpatched modem.sys, ModemHandleSymbolicLink allocates a 0x202-byte PagedPool buffer (tag 'UMDM') for the PDO name via ExAllocatePoolWithTag and passes it to IoGetDeviceProperty(DevicePropertyPhysicalDeviceObjectName) without zero-initializing it. IoGetDeviceProperty writes only the actual PDO-name bytes plus a terminator, leaving the tail of the allocation uninitialized. The sibling buffer (Destination, for the '\DosDevices\' prefix + friendly name) IS zeroed via memset in both builds; the second allocation was omitted. The patch inserts memset(DeviceName.Buffer, 0, DeviceName.MaximumLength + 2) = memset(buffer, 0, 0x202) between the allocation and the IoGetDeviceProperty call. This is a CWE-908 hardening change: the uninitialized tail is never disclosed, because DeviceName.Length is set from ResultLength, IoCreateSymbolicLink copies only Length bytes as the link target, and the buffer is freed immediately. No code reads the bytes beyond Length or returns them to a caller-observable location.
security_findings.trigger_conditions:
1. A modem device must be enumerated so the PnP Manager invokes ModemAddDevice (e.g., plugging in a modem, or a privileged 'pnputil /scan-devices' / 'devcon rescan' / disable-re-enable cycle).
2. ModemAddDevice or ModemPnP calls ModemHandleSymbolicLink with the create flag, which allocates DeviceName.Buffer and, in the unpatched build, passes it to IoGetDeviceProperty without zeroing.
3. There is no untrusted request path (IOCTL, etc.) that reaches this code; the trigger is device enumeration, a privileged/physical action.
security_findings.primitive: None demonstrable. The uninitialized tail of the 0x202-byte buffer is never read out: IoCreateSymbolicLink uses only DeviceName.Length bytes and the buffer is freed immediately afterward. The change is defense-in-depth hardening (zero-before-use) consistent with the sibling buffer, not the closure of a reachable information-disclosure primitive.
security_findings.assembly:
Full UNPATCHED assembly of ModemHandleSymbolicLink @ 0x1C00076CC:
00000001C00076CC  mov     [rsp-18h+arg_0], rbx
00000001C00076D1  mov     [rsp-18h+arg_8], rsi
00000001C00076D6  mov     [rsp-18h+arg_10], rdi
00000001C00076DB  push    rbp
00000001C00076DC  push    r14
00000001C00076DE  push    r15
00000001C00076E0  mov     rbp, rsp
00000001C00076E3  sub     rsp, 50h
00000001C00076E7  xor     r15d, r15d
00000001C00076EA  mov     dword ptr [rbp+Destination.Length], 2000000h
00000001C00076F1  mov     r14d, r8d                         ; r14 = arg3 (create/delete flag)
00000001C00076F4  mov     [rbp+arg_18], r15d               ; ResultLength = 0
00000001C00076F8  mov     rbx, rdx                          ; rbx = arg2 (UNICODE_STRING*)
00000001C00076FB  mov     rsi, rcx                          ; rsi = arg1 (PDO)
; --- First allocation (Destination buffer) ---
00000001C00076FE  mov     edx, 202h                        ; NumberOfBytes
00000001C0007703  mov     r8d, 4D444D55h                   ; Tag 'UMDM'
00000001C0007709  lea     ecx, [r15+1]                     ; PoolType = PagedPool
00000001C000770D  call    cs:__imp_ExAllocatePoolWithTag
00000001C0007714  nop     dword ptr [rax+rax+00h]
00000001C0007719  mov     [rbp+Destination.Buffer], rax
00000001C000771D  test    rax, rax
00000001C0007720  jnz     short loc_1C000772C
00000001C0007722  mov     eax, 0C000009Ah                  ; STATUS_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES
00000001C0007727  jmp     loc_1C00078C3
; --- First buffer IS zeroed ---
00000001C000772C  movzx   r8d, [rbp+Destination.MaximumLength]  ; Size
00000001C0007731  xor     edx, edx                         ; Val
00000001C0007733  mov     rcx, rax
00000001C0007736  call    memset                           ; memset(Destination.Buffer, 0, 0x200)
00000001C000773B  lea     rdx, Source                      ; "\DosDevices\"
00000001C0007742  lea     rcx, [rbp+Destination]
00000001C0007746  call    cs:__imp_RtlAppendUnicodeToString
00000001C000774D  nop     dword ptr [rax+rax+00h]
00000001C0007752  mov     rax, [rbp+Destination.Buffer]
00000001C0007756  mov     edx, 9                           ; DevicePropertyFriendlyName
00000001C000775B  movzx   ecx, [rbp+Destination.Length]
00000001C000775F  movzx   r8d, [rbp+Destination.MaximumLength]
00000001C0007764  shr     rcx, 1
00000001C0007767  lea     r9, [rax+rcx*2]                  ; PropertyBuffer
00000001C000776B  movzx   eax, [rbp+Destination.Length]
00000001C000776F  sub     r8d, eax                         ; BufferLength
00000001C0007772  mov     rcx, rsi                         ; DeviceObject
00000001C0007775  lea     rax, [rbp+arg_18]
00000001C0007779  mov     [rsp+50h+ResultLength], rax
00000001C000777E  call    cs:__imp_IoGetDeviceProperty     ; FriendlyName
00000001C0007785  nop     dword ptr [rax+rax+00h]
00000001C000778A  mov     edi, eax
00000001C000778C  test    eax, eax
00000001C000778E  js      loc_1C00078AF
00000001C0007794  movzx   eax, word ptr [rbp+arg_18]       ; ResultLength
00000001C0007798  sub     ax, 2
00000001C000779C  add     [rbp+Destination.Length], ax
00000001C00077A0  test    r14d, r14d                       ; check arg3
00000001C00077A3  jz      loc_1C0007877                     ; arg3==0 -> DELETE path
; --- arg3 != 0: CREATE path ---
00000001C00077A9  mov     edx, 202h                        ; NumberOfBytes
00000001C00077AE  mov     dword ptr [rbp+DeviceName.Length], 2000000h
00000001C00077B5  mov     ecx, 1                           ; PoolType = PagedPool
00000001C00077BA  mov     r8d, 4D444D55h                   ; Tag 'UMDM'
00000001C00077C0  call    cs:__imp_ExAllocatePoolWithTag   ; 2nd allocation
00000001C00077C7  nop     dword ptr [rax+rax+00h]
00000001C00077CC  mov     [rbp+DeviceName.Buffer], rax
00000001C00077D0  test    rax, rax
00000001C00077D3  jnz     short loc_1C00077DF
00000001C00077D5  mov     edi, 0C000009Ah
00000001C00077DA  jmp     loc_1C00078AF
; *** UNPATCHED: no memset here - buffer tail stays uninitialized ***
00000001C00077DF  movzx   r8d, [rbp+DeviceName.MaximumLength]  ; BufferLength = 0x200
00000001C00077E4  lea     rcx, [rbp+arg_18]
00000001C00077E8  mov     [rsp+50h+ResultLength], rcx
00000001C00077ED  mov     r9, rax                          ; PropertyBuffer (uninitialized)
00000001C00077F0  mov     rcx, rsi                         ; DeviceObject
00000001C00077F3  mov     edx, 0Bh                         ; DevicePropertyPhysicalDeviceObjectName
00000001C00077F8  call    cs:__imp_IoGetDeviceProperty
00000001C00077FF  nop     dword ptr [rax+rax+00h]
00000001C0007804  test    eax, eax
00000001C0007806  js      loc_1C00078AD
00000001C000780C  movzx   eax, word ptr [rbp+arg_18]       ; ResultLength
00000001C0007810  lea     rdx, [rbp+DeviceName]
00000001C0007814  sub     ax, 2
00000001C0007818  lea     rcx, [rbp+Destination]
00000001C000781C  add     [rbp+DeviceName.Length], ax      ; Length = ResultLength - 2
00000001C0007820  call    cs:__imp_IoCreateSymbolicLink    ; uses DeviceName.Length bytes only
00000001C0007827  nop     dword ptr [rax+rax+00h]
00000001C000782C  mov     rcx, [rbp+DeviceName.Buffer]
00000001C0007830  xor     edx, edx
00000001C0007832  call    cs:__imp_ExFreePoolWithTag       ; free DeviceName.Buffer
00000001C0007839  nop     dword ptr [rax+rax+00h]
00000001C000783E  mov     r9, rbx
00000001C0007841  mov     [rbp+DeviceName.Buffer], r15
00000001C0007845  xor     r8d, r8d
00000001C0007848  lea     rdx, GUID_DEVINTERFACE_MODEM
00000001C000784F  mov     rcx, rsi
00000001C0007852  call    cs:__imp_IoRegisterDeviceInterface
00000001C0007859  nop     dword ptr [rax+rax+00h]
00000001C000785E  mov     edi, eax
00000001C0007860  test    eax, eax
00000001C0007862  js      short loc_1C00078AF
00000001C0007864  mov     dl, 1                            ; Enable
00000001C0007866  mov     rcx, rbx
00000001C0007869  call    cs:__imp_IoSetDeviceInterfaceState
00000001C0007870  nop     dword ptr [rax+rax+00h]
00000001C0007875  jmp     short loc_1C00078AF
; --- Cleanup/epilogue ---
00000001C00078AF  mov     rcx, [rbp+Destination.Buffer]
00000001C00078B3  xor     edx, edx
00000001C00078B5  call    cs:__imp_ExFreePoolWithTag
00000001C00078BC  nop     dword ptr [rax+rax+00h]
00000001C00078C1  mov     eax, edi
00000001C00078C3  lea     r11, [rsp+50h+var_s0]
00000001C00078C8  mov     rbx, [r11+20h]
00000001C00078CC  mov     rsi, [r11+28h]
00000001C00078D0  mov     rdi, [r11+30h]
00000001C00078D4  mov     rsp, r11
00000001C00078D7  pop     r15
00000001C00078D9  pop     r14
00000001C00078DB  pop     rbp
00000001C00078DC  retn

breakpoints.why: Function entry. arg1 (rcx) = PDO DEVICE_OBJECT*, arg2 (rdx) = UNICODE_STRING* for the device interface symbolic link, arg3 (r8) = create/delete flag (nonzero = create path). Verify r8 to confirm the create path.

Second ExAllocatePoolWithTag call for DeviceName.Buffer. After return, rax holds the newly allocated 0x202-byte pool buffer (uninitialized in the unpatched build). db rax L202 shows the pool contents.

In the unpatched build, execution reaches here directly after the allocation with no memset; r9 holds the buffer pointer, still uninitialized. In the patched build, memset (0x1C00077EE) runs before this point. Useful to confirm the buffer is not zeroed in the unpatched build.

IoCreateSymbolicLink(&Destination, &DeviceName). Only DeviceName.Length bytes are used as the link target; the tail of DeviceName.Buffer is not consumed. Inspect DeviceName at [rbp+DeviceName]: Length, MaximumLength (0x200), Buffer.

watch: r8d at entry: create/delete flag; nonzero selects the create path that exercises the second allocation
rax after 0x1C00077C0 (ExAllocatePoolWithTag return): the 0x202-byte DeviceName.Buffer - db rax L202 shows uninitialized contents in the unpatched build
key_offsets: 0x1C00077C0: second ExAllocatePoolWithTag - allocates the 0x202-byte DeviceName buffer
0x1C00077DF (unpatched): proceeds to set up IoGetDeviceProperty args with no zeroing of the buffer
0x1C0007814: sub ax, 0x2 - computes ResultLength-2 for DeviceName.Length (present in both builds)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup:
1. Load the unpatched modem.sys (Unimodem driver installed and active).
2. Trigger AddDevice for a modem device: present a modem, or use 'pnputil /scan-devices' / 'devcon rescan' to force PnP re-enumeration, or disable/re-enable a modem in Device Manager.
3. ModemAddDevice (0x1C0006B60) calls ModemHandleSymbolicLink with the create flag.
4. To observe the uninitialized buffer, break at 0x1C00077DF (after the second allocation succeeds, before IoGetDeviceProperty) and dump rax for 0x202 bytes.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: At 0x1C00077DF in the unpatched build, rax points to a 0x202-byte pool block whose contents are not all zero (db rax L202). After IoGetDeviceProperty, only the PDO-name bytes are written; the remainder stays as prior pool contents. In the patched build the same region is all zeros after the memset at 0x1C00077EE. Note the uninitialized tail is not exposed outside the kernel in either build: IoCreateSymbolicLink uses only DeviceName.Length bytes and the buffer is freed immediately.
debugging_aids.notes: The memset helper is at 0x1C0001A40. ModemHandleSymbolicLink is also called from ModemPnP (0x1C0006EF0); the delete path (arg3==0) does not use the second buffer and is unaffected. Only the create path (arg3!=0) exercises the second allocation. The first buffer (Destination, for the \DosDevices\ prefix + friendly name) is zeroed via memset in both builds; the developer added the same zeroing for the second allocation in the patch. Pool tag is 'UMDM' (0x4D444D55). The buffer is freed at 0x1C0007832 after IoCreateSymbolicLink.

summary: The patch adds a single memset (0x1C0001A40) that zero-initializes the second 0x202-byte PagedPool buffer (DeviceName.Buffer, PDO name) allocated in ModemHandleSymbolicLink (0x1C00076CC) before it is passed to IoGetDeviceProperty(DevicePropertyPhysicalDeviceObjectName). The sibling buffer was already zeroed in both builds. This is CWE-908 (Use of Uninitialized Resource) hardening. No information disclosure is demonstrable: DeviceName.Length is set from ResultLength, IoCreateSymbolicLink consumes only Length bytes as the link target, and the buffer is freed immediately, so the uninitialized tail is never read or exposed. The path runs only during PnP device setup/enumeration (ModemAddDevice at 0x1C0006B60), not from an untrusted request. Severity: Low (defense-in-depth).
